claim_boundary: The review classifies current contraction candidates; it does not change code or prove post-refactor parity.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_inventory.summary: Run the tracked architecture-reduction owner tests and known-bad cases.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Confirm shrink-oriented scope and the observable behavior that must remain unchanged.
Map existing model ownership, code boundaries, state, side effects, entrypoints, and compatibility surfaces.
Classify merge, collapse, remove, keep-facade, or review candidates with proof status.
Run the native reduction model and reject candidates lacking equivalence, facade, or disposition evidence.
Return ready/scoped/risky/blocked candidates and typed implementation/parity handoffs.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Classify behavior-preserving architecture contraction candidates and their proof/handoff status.

do_not_use_when: The task is greenfield structure planning, direct production refactoring, or intended behavior change.
routes.summary: Classify behavior-preserving architecture contraction candidates and their proof/handoff status.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-architecture-reduction skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Production edits and public-entrypoint parity remain owned by StructureMesh and DevelopmentProcessFlow.
Every candidate must be grounded in an observable contract and model-to-code mapping.
Only equivalence or current public-facade proof can make a contraction ready.
Accept existing-model ownership plus code mapping; classify contraction candidates; block missing equivalence/facade evidence; hand ready public-entrypoint work to Code Structure Recommenda.
Map FunctionBlocks, state, side effects, public entrypoints, and contraction candidates.
Public entrypoints require StructureMesh parity, duplicate generators route to ContractExhaustionMesh, and new/deepened models require template harvest closure.
Route id: architecture_reduction; role: public_owner; native owner: architecture_reduction. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns reduction proof, not implementation.
Use for merge, collapse, remove, keep-facade, or manual-review decisions over duplicate handlers, modules, adapters, branches, fields, or validations.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard gates the native reduction review and cannot refactor code or invent equivalence.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Only equivalence or current facade proof can make a contraction ready; risky, scoped, stale, and property-only candidates remain visible.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus contraction candidates and proof status.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when an existing FlowGuard model and code map should drive behavior-preserving architecture contraction, including merg.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not use for greenfield module planning, direct refactoring, or behavior change; return unclear models to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Ground existing ownership and declare an ObservableArchitectureContract.
Record proof status, target action, compatibility disposition, risks, and required next route.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
